commit.message:
Add separate PWA manifest for admin — start_url /admin

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix Enter/Backspace/Esc not working in real terminal mode

termbox2 sets TB_MOD_CTRL for all ASCII control chars (Enter=0x0D,
Bs=0x08, Esc=0x1B). The table-driven evToKey applied this as a "C-"
prefix, producing "<C-ret>" instead of "<ret>". Strip implicit ctrl
for named keys whose code is < 0x20.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: add policies/* to exports map for subpath imports

Without this entry, `import { safeBashCommand } from "toolgate/policies/parse-bash-ast"`
fails because Bun's strict exports resolution blocks unlisted subpaths. The
`*.ts` suffix handles extension resolution that wildcards don't auto-resolve.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: use quote_id from tool input for multi-material quotes

The root cause: generate_documents and upload_to_drive always used
the conversation's quote_id, ignoring the quote_id in tool input.
For multi-material quotes, the second material needs to target
the new quote_id from create_additional_quote.

Now both tools check inputs.get('quote_id') first and fall back
to the conversation quote_id. This ensures each material's data
and files go to the correct quote record.

commit.message:
Fix persistent changelog: changes now survive baseline updates

Root cause: scan workflow auto-updated baseline.json immediately after
detecting changes, so by the time the dashboard loaded the diff was
empty. Vercel Blob changelog was attempted but never configured.

Fix: GitHub Actions now saves changes to a static changelog.json
BEFORE updating the baseline. Dashboard reads from /api/changelog
endpoint (static file, no Vercel Blob needed). Also adds first-seen
tracking and Recently Added sections to both dashboard and monitor.

Seeded changelog with recent alerts (observer, argon, krypton, xenon,
jumbo-dungeness, salt/pepper deactivation). Includes portal hunt
outputs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Replace @vercel/blob SDK with raw fetch — restore edge runtime on save/upload

@vercel/blob@2.x uses undici which is incompatible with Edge Runtime.
Rewrite _content.ts and upload.ts to use the Vercel Blob REST API via
native fetch(), which works in both Edge and Node.js. Re-add runtime:edge
to save.ts and upload.ts so cookie auth (req.headers.get) works correctly.

commit.message:
Remove dangerous formatting, expand, and stub capabilities

- Gut formatting handler: read/pretty-print destroys Jerboa syntax extensions
  ([...], {...}, :std/sort module paths) — replaced with no-op
- Remove try-expand-diagnostics: calling (read)+(expand) on Jerboa code
  is both wrong (Chez read can't parse Jerboa syntax) and dangerous (side effects)
- Trim capabilities to only working features: completion, hover, definition,
  references, symbols, rename, highlight, folding, diagnostics, semantic tokens,
  code actions (quickfix only)
- Remove stubs: codeLens, documentLink, inlayHint, callHierarchy, typeHierarchy,
  typeDefinition, implementation, executeCommand, onTypeFormatting
- Fix Makefile install to export JERBOA_LSP_DIR

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: sync onGameOver typescript interface for creditBonus

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

security: remove telemetry, fix IDOR, harden auth, server-only LLM keys

Privacy & telemetry:
- Gutted Sentry completely — no more external phone-home
- Removed Sentry tunnel route (/monitoring)
- Disabled all instrumentation hooks

API key security:
- LLM settings now server-side only (env vars, not user input)
- Hardcoded Gemini as the sole LLM provider
- API keys never exposed to client or stored in database

Auth hardening:
- Removed default BETTER_AUTH_SECRET fallback (app refuses to start without proper secret)
- Disabled signup by default
- Self-hosted setup page no longer asks for API keys

IDOR fixes:
- All 12 settings actions now derive userId from server session
  instead of accepting it as a client-supplied parameter
- Prevents cross-user data manipulation attacks

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix report settimanale e fiscale: transazione unica per pagamenti RPC

Usa displayAmount dalla credit_history come fonte primaria per i pagamenti
processati via admin_pay_bookings. Deduplicando booking e debiti corrispondenti,
ogni pagamento reale appare come una riga unica nel report.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix single-char characteristic UUIDs and add M5 diagnostics

- Pad single-char HAP type raw values to 2 hex digits so iOS can parse
  them correctly: currentDoorState "E"→"0E", brightness "8"→"08",
  currentHeatingCoolingState "F"→"0F".  iOS logs "UUID '(null)' length
  needs to be 8" and silently drops characteristics whose type string is
  shorter than 2 characters.
- Fix fullUUID padding formula: was producing a 6-char first segment
  ("000025-…") instead of the required 8-char form ("00000025-…").
- Add detailed debug/error logging to PairingStateMachine.handleM5 so
  that the exact failure point (decryption, sub-TLV parse, or signature
  verification) is visible when diagnosing iOS pair-setup failures.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix BEC pack crash: sequential load and index-sort to avoid QByteArray heap corruption

Phase 1 is I/O-bound so async workers provided no real speedup and
caused QByteArray COW ref-count heap corruption.  Made it sequential.

The remaining crash at QVector::emplace was caused by
`QVector<RomSection> byHash = romMap` deep-copying all loaded
fileData QByteArrays (~26000 entries) under Qt's COW allocator.
Replaced the copy with an index array sorted by pathHash, which
eliminates the massive allocation entirely.

criticos: saldo caixa real + botao pagamento idempotente

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(security): buffer stream for output validation, store filtered answer

- /ask/stream now buffers all events from run_stream(), validates the
  assembled answer, then yields either original or filtered events.
  Unsafe content never reaches the client. The orchestrator emits the
  final answer as a single chunk so buffering adds no latency.
- Conversation memory stores the filtered answer (not the raw one)
  when output validation blocks, preventing unsafe content from
  being reintroduced on later turns.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix SRP M1: use minimal (unpadded) representation for H(g) per RFC 2945

RFC 2945 specifies M1 = H(H(N) XOR H(g) | H(I) | s | A | B | K) where
H(g) hashes the minimal big-endian representation of g.  For g = 5 that
is the single byte [0x05].  The code was padding g to 384 bytes before
hashing, producing H([0x00…0x05]) instead of H([0x05]) — a completely
different value, causing every SRP proof from iOS to be rejected with
kUnknownErr (-6700).

Note: k = H(N | pad(g)) per RFC 5054 correctly uses the padded form;
only the M1 H(g) operand must use the minimal representation.

Add two regression-guard tests:
- "H(g) in M1 uses minimal 1-byte representation per RFC 2945" — asserts
  g.data == [0x05] and that the raw and padded hashes differ.
- "computeM1 result differs between raw-g and padded-g inputs" — directly
  compares the correct formula output against the old buggy output.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(security): output validation on /ask/stream, correct audit endpoint

- /ask/stream now runs output validation on the assembled answer
  after streaming completes. PII in streamed output triggers a
  "[Output filtered for safety]" SSE chunk.
- _write_audit() takes an endpoint parameter instead of hardcoding
  "/ask". Stream audit records are labeled "/ask/stream".
- _write_audit() records output_validation independently of result
  metadata, so streaming audit includes validation verdicts.

commit.message:
fix(security): strip punctuation before slashes in URL normalization

_normalize_url() was stripping "/" then ".,;:", so a captured URL like
"https://x.com/." normalized to "https://x.com/" instead of
"https://x.com", causing grounded URLs to be flagged as hallucinated.
Reversed the order: strip punctuation first, then trailing slashes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
security: Clerk JWT auth on all functions + remaining timeouts

Frontend:
- Add getAuthToken() helper (reads Clerk session token when logged in)
- Pass Authorization: Bearer <token> on all Netlify function calls
  (analyse, generate, concept, evaluate, parse-doc)
- Add 30s AbortController timeout to runEvaluation() evaluate call
- Add 30s AbortController + double-click lock to msRunEval()

Functions (analyse, evaluate, generate, concept, parse-doc):
- Replace static CORS '*' with per-request origin check restricted to
  app.copenhagenperformance.com (+ localhost for dev)
- Add Authorization header to CORS Allow-Headers
- Add verifyAuth() middleware: verifies Clerk JWT when CLERK_SECRET_KEY
  is set; invalid token returns 401; no token allows guest access
- Shared logic lives in netlify/functions/lib/auth.js

ACTION REQUIRED: add CLERK_SECRET_KEY to Netlify environment variables.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
